1→# Bot Telegram MyPocket — Plano de Implementação
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→Integrar um bot Telegram ao MyPocket para registrar despesas, importar faturas CSV e consultar orçamentos/gastos via chat. Inclui deduplicação centralizada para evitar transações duplicadas tanto no bot quanto no app web.
     6→
     7→## Current State Analysis
     8→
     9→- **Sem deduplicação:** importar o mesmo CSV duas vezes cria duplicatas (`src/app/api/import/route.ts:166-186` cria transações incondicionalmente)
    10→- **Sem integração Telegram:** nenhum modelo, endpoint ou lógica de bot existe
    11→- **Settings extensível:** página de settings (`src/app/settings/page.tsx:528-554`) usa tabs Radix — nova aba é direta
    12→- **Middleware restritivo:** `src/auth.config.ts:11-28` bloqueia todas as rotas não-auth — webhook precisa de exceção
    13→- **Summary endpoint reutilizável:** `GET /api/summary` retorna totais, breakdown por categoria e alertas de orçamento — ideal para consultas do bot
    14→- **Categorizer reutilizável:** `src/lib/categorizer.ts:95-109` faz auto-categorização por keywords — bot pode usar diretamente
    15→- **CSV parser reutilizável:** `src/lib/csv-parser.ts:151` detecta banco e parseia — bot pode chamar diretamente
    16→
    17→## Desired End State
    18→
    19→1. Módulo de deduplicação centralizado previne transações duplicatas em toda a aplicação
    20→2. Bot Telegram funcional com: vinculação por deep link, registro de despesas via texto, importação CSV, consultas de resumo/categorias/transações
    21→3. Nova aba "Telegram" na página de settings com botão de vincular/desvincular
    22→4. Webhook seguro integrado ao Next.js sem dependências externas pesadas
    23→
    24→**Verificação:** Enviar mensagem de texto com dados de transação no Telegram → transação aparece no dashboard web. Importar mesmo CSV via bot e via web → sem duplicatas.
    25→
    26→## What We're NOT Doing
    27→
    28→- Interface de IA/NLP — apenas comandos e formatos estruturados
    29→- Notificações proativas do bot (alertas de orçamento, lembretes)
    30→- Suporte a WhatsApp ou outros mensageiros
    31→- OCR de prints/fotos via bot (apenas texto e CSV)
    32→- Paginação server-side no endpoint de transações existente
    33→

    39→|----------|-----------|
    40→| `ai_docs/brainstorm/2026-03-07-telegram-bot-design.md` | Design validado com todas as decisões |

    46→
    47→**Scope Warning:** Este plano cobre o bot Telegram, dedup, e a aba de settings. Não assume comportamento de investments, recurring expenses, ou OCR.
    48→
    49→---
    50→
    51→## Risks & Mitigations
    52→
    53→| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
    54→|------|------------|--------|------------|
    55→| Telegram webhook não alcançável em dev local | H | M | Usar ngrok/localtunnel para expor URL local; endpoint de setup aceita URL customizada |
    56→| Dedup falso positivo (pular transação legítima) | L | H | Match exato (description case-insensitive + amount + date mesmo dia) — conservador por design |
    57→| Middleware bloqueia webhook requests | M | H | Adicionar exceção explícita em auth.config.ts; testar com request sem session |
    58→| Telegram File API timeout para CSVs grandes | L | M | Limitar tamanho de arquivo (20MB — limite do Telegram); timeout generoso no fetch |
    59→| Schema migration quebra banco existente | L | H | Novos modelos apenas (additive) — sem alteração em modelos existentes |

1→# Phase 1: Schema + Deduplicação
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→Adicionar modelos Prisma para vinculação Telegram, criar módulo centralizado de deduplicação de transações, e integrá-lo ao fluxo de importação existente. Esta fase entrega valor imediato — deduplicação funciona no app web independente do bot.
     6→
     7→## Reference Docs for This Phase
     8→- `prisma/schema.prisma` (lines 16-49) - User model com relations
     9→- `prisma/schema.prisma` (lines 414-435) - Final do schema (onde adicionar novos modelos)
    10→- `src/app/api/import/route.ts` (lines 80-187) - Loop de criação de transações
    11→- `src/app/api/import/route.ts` (lines 241-263) - Response builder
    12→- `src/lib/db.ts` - Prisma client singleton
    13→
    14→## Changes Required
    15→
    16→#### 1. Add TelegramLink and TelegramLinkToken models to Prisma schema
    17→
    18→**File**: `prisma/schema.prisma` (MODIFY)
    19→**Complexity**: Low
    20→**TDD**: NO — schema definition, no logic
    21→**Depends On**: none
    22→
    23→**Load Before Implementing**:
    24→1. `prisma/schema.prisma` (lines 16-49) - User model para adicionar relations
    25→2. `prisma/schema.prisma` (lines 414-435) - Final do arquivo onde inserir novos modelos
    26→
    27→**Pre-conditions**:
    28→- [ ] Prisma schema exists at `prisma/schema.prisma`
    29→- [ ] User model exists with `id` field of type `String`
    30→
    31→**Why**: Necessário para vincular contas Telegram a usuários e gerar tokens temporários para deep link.
    32→
    33→**Acceptance Criteria**:
    34→```gherkin
    35→Given the Prisma schema with existing models
    36→When the new TelegramLink and TelegramLinkToken models are added
    37→Then `npm run db:generate` succeeds without errors

    40→
    41→**Implementation**:
    42→

    44→

    53→
    54→```prisma
    55→// ==========================================
    56→// TELEGRAM INTEGRATION MODELS

   105→
   106→#### 2. Create deduplication module with tests
   107→
   108→**File**: `src/lib/dedup.ts` (CREATE) + `src/lib/dedup.test.ts` (CREATE)
   109→**Complexity**: High
   110→**TDD**: YES
   111→**Depends On**: Task 1 (Prisma schema must have Transaction model)
   112→
   113→**Load Before Implementing**:
   114→1. `prisma/schema.prisma` (lines 100-137) - Transaction model fields
   115→2. `src/lib/db.ts` (full file) - Prisma client singleton import
   116→3. `src/lib/categorizer.ts` (lines 1-10) - Import pattern example
   117→
   118→**Pre-conditions**:
   119→- [ ] Task 1 completed (schema generated)
   120→- [ ] Transaction model has `description`, `amount`, `date`, `userId`, `deletedAt` fields
   121→
   122→**Why**: Lógica centralizada para detectar transações duplicatas. Usada pelo import web, pelo bot (registro avulso), e pela importação CSV via bot.
   123→
   124→**Acceptance Criteria**:
   125→```gherkin
   126→Given a transaction exists with description "PIX REST FULANO", amount -45.90, date 2026-03-05
   127→When findDuplicate is called with same description (case-insensitive), same amount, same date
   128→Then it returns the existing transaction
   129→
   130→Given a transaction exists with description "PIX REST FULANO", amount -45.90, date 2026-03-05
   131→When findDuplicate is called with different description "UBER TRIP"
   132→Then it returns null
   133→
   134→Given a transaction exists but is soft-deleted (deletedAt is set)
   135→When findDuplicate is called with matching description, amount, date
   136→Then it returns null (ignores deleted transactions)
   137→
   138→Given a transaction with description "PIX REST FULANO", amount -45.90, date 2026-03-05T15:30:00
   139→When findDuplicate is called with same description, same amount, date 2026-03-05T08:00:00
   140→Then it returns the existing transaction (same day, different time)
   141→```
   142→
   143→**Implementation**:
   144→
   145→`src/lib/dedup.ts`:
   146→```typescript
   147→import prisma from "@/lib/db"
   148→
   149→export interface DuplicateCheckParams {
   150→  userId: string
   151→  description: string
   152→  amount: number
   153→  date: Date
   154→}
   155→
   156→export async function findDuplicate({
   157→  userId,
   158→  description,
   159→  amount,
   160→  date,
   161→}: DuplicateCheckParams) {
   162→  // Normalize date to start/end of day
   163→  const dayStart = new Date(date)
   164→  dayStart.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0)
   165→  const dayEnd = new Date(date)
   166→  dayEnd.setHours(23, 59, 59, 999)
   167→
   168→  const duplicate = await prisma.transaction.findFirst({
   169→    where: {
   170→      userId,
   171→      description: {
   172→        equals: description.trim(),
   173→        mode: "insensitive",
   174→      },
   175→      amount,
   176→      date: {
   177→        gte: dayStart,
   178→        lte: dayEnd,
   179→      },
   180→      deletedAt: null,
   181→    },
   182→  })
   183→
   184→  return duplicate
   185→}
   186→
   187→export async function filterDuplicates(
   188→  userId: string,
   189→  transactions: Array<{ description: string; amount: number; date: Date }>
   190→): Promise<{ unique: typeof transactions; duplicateCount: number }> {
   191→  const unique: typeof transactions = []
   192→  let duplicateCount = 0
   193→
   194→  for (const t of transactions) {
   195→    const dup = await findDuplicate({
   196→      userId,
   197→      description: t.description,
   198→      amount: t.amount,
   199→      date: t.date,
   200→    })
   201→    if (dup) {
   202→      duplicateCount++
   203→    } else {
   204→      unique.push(t)
   205→    }
   206→  }
   207→
   208→  return { unique, duplicateCount }
   209→}
   210→```
   211→
   212→`src/lib/dedup.test.ts`:
   213→```typescript
   214→import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from "vitest"
   215→import { findDuplicate, filterDuplicates } from "./dedup"
   216→
   217→// Mock prisma
   218→vi.mock("@/lib/db", () => ({
   219→  default: {
   220→    transaction: {
   221→      findFirst: vi.fn(),
   222→    },
   223→  },
   224→}))
   225→
   226→import prisma from "@/lib/db"
   227→
   228→const mockFindFirst = vi.mocked(prisma.transaction.findFirst)
   229→
   230→describe("findDuplicate", () => {
   231→  beforeEach(() => {
   232→    vi.clearAllMocks()
   233→  })
   234→
   235→  it("returns matching transaction when duplicate exists", async () => {
   236→    const existingTx = {
   237→      id: "tx1",
   238→      description: "PIX REST FULANO",
   239→      amount: -45.9,
   240→      date: new Date("2026-03-05T15:30:00"),
   241→    }
   242→    mockFindFirst.mockResolvedValue(existingTx as never)
   243→
   244→    const result = await findDuplicate({
   245→      userId: "user1",
   246→      description: "PIX REST FULANO",
   247→      amount: -45.9,
   248→      date: new Date("2026-03-05T08:00:00"),
   249→    })
   250→
   251→    expect(result).toEqual(existingTx)
   252→    expect(mockFindFirst).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
   253→      where: {
   254→        userId: "user1",
   255→        description: {
   256→          equals: "PIX REST FULANO",
   257→          mode: "insensitive",
   258→        },
   259→        amount: -45.9,
   260→        date: {
   261→          gte: expect.any(Date),
   262→          lte: expect.any(Date),
   263→        },
   264→        deletedAt: null,
   265→      },
   266→    })
   267→  })
   268→
   269→  it("returns null when no duplicate exists", async () => {
   270→    mockFindFirst.mockResolvedValue(null)
   271→
   272→    const result = await findDuplicate({
   273→      userId: "user1",
   274→      description: "UBER TRIP",
   275→      amount: -23.5,
   276→      date: new Date("2026-03-05"),
   277→    })
   278→
   279→    expect(result).toBeNull()
   280→  })
   281→
   282→  it("trims description before comparing", async () => {
   283→    mockFindFirst.mockResolvedValue(null)
   284→
   285→    await findDuplicate({
   286→      userId: "user1",
   287→      description: "  PIX REST FULANO  ",
   288→      amount: -45.9,
   289→      date: new Date("2026-03-05"),
   290→    })
   291→
   292→    expect(mockFindFirst).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
   293→      expect.objectContaining({
   294→        where: expect.objectContaining({
   295→          description: {
   296→            equals: "PIX REST FULANO",
   297→            mode: "insensitive",
   298→          },
   299→        }),
   300→      })
   301→    )
   302→  })
   303→
   304→  it("uses day boundaries for date comparison", async () => {
   305→    mockFindFirst.mockResolvedValue(null)
   306→
   307→    await findDuplicate({
   308→      userId: "user1",
   309→      description: "TEST",
   310→      amount: -10,
   311→      date: new Date("2026-03-05T14:30:00"),
   312→    })
   313→
   314→    const call = mockFindFirst.mock.calls[0][0]
   315→    const dateFilter = call?.where?.date as { gte: Date; lte: Date }
   316→    expect(dateFilter.gte.getHours()).toBe(0)
   317→    expect(dateFilter.gte.getMinutes()).toBe(0)
   318→    expect(dateFilter.lte.getHours()).toBe(23)
   319→    expect(dateFilter.lte.getMinutes()).toBe(59)
   320→  })
   321→})
   322→
   323→describe("filterDuplicates", () => {
   324→  beforeEach(() => {
   325→    vi.clearAllMocks()
   326→  })
   327→
   328→  it("separates unique and duplicate transactions", async () => {
   329→    mockFindFirst
   330→      .mockResolvedValueOnce({ id: "existing" } as never) // first is dup
   331→      .mockResolvedValueOnce(null) // second is unique
   332→
   333→    const transactions = [
   334→      { description: "DUP", amount: -10, date: new Date("2026-03-05") },
   335→      { description: "NEW", amount: -20, date: new Date("2026-03-05") },
   336→    ]
   337→
   338→    const result = await filterDuplicates("user1", transactions)
   339→
   340→    expect(result.unique).toHaveLength(1)

   356→#### 3. Integrate deduplication into import endpoint
   357→

    58→`src/lib/telegram/parser.ts`:
    59→```typescript
    60→export interface ParsedExpense {
    61→  description: string
    62→  amount: number
    63→  date: Date
    64→}
    65→
    66→export function parseExpenseMessage(text: string): ParsedExpense | null {
    67→  const trimmed = text.trim()
    68→  if (!trimmed) return null
    69→
    70→  // We'll track parts to remove by position
    71→  const removals: Array<{ start: number; end: number }> = []
    72→
    73→  // Extract date (DD/MM/YYYY) — look for last occurrence
    74→  const dateRegex = /(\d{2})\/(\d{2})\/(\d{4})/g
    75→  let dateMatch: RegExpExecArray | null = null
    76→  let lastDateMatch: RegExpExecArray | null = null
    77→  while ((dateMatch = dateRegex.exec(trimmed)) !== null) {
    78→    lastDateMatch = dateMatch
    79→  }
    80→
    81→  let date: Date
    82→  if (lastDateMatch) {
    83→    const [, day, month, year] = lastDateMatch
    84→    date = new Date(parseInt(year), parseInt(month) - 1, parseInt(day), 12, 0, 0)
    85→    removals.push({ start: lastDateMatch.index, end: lastDateMatch.index + lastDateMatch[0].length })
    86→  } else {
    87→    const now = new Date()
    88→    date = new Date(now.getFullYear(), now.getMonth(), now.getDate(), 12, 0, 0)
    89→  }
    90→
    91→  // Extract amount — look for Brazilian number format (last occurrence)
    92→  // Matches: 45,90 | 1.234,56 | 1234,56
    93→  const amountRegex = /(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{3})*,\d{2}|\d+,\d{2})/g
    94→  let amountMatch: RegExpExecArray | null = null
    95→  let lastAmountMatch: RegExpExecArray | null = null
    96→  while ((amountMatch = amountRegex.exec(trimmed)) !== null) {
    97→    lastAmountMatch = amountMatch
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  if (!lastAmountMatch) return null
   101→
   102→  const amountStr = lastAmountMatch[1]
   103→  const amount = parseFloat(amountStr.replace(/\./g, "").replace(",", "."))
   104→  if (isNaN(amount) || amount <= 0) return null
   105→
   106→  removals.push({ start: lastAmountMatch.index, end: lastAmountMatch.index + lastAmountMatch[0].length })
   107→
   108→  // Build description by removing matched parts by position (sorted descending to preserve indices)
   109→  let description = trimmed
   110→  removals.sort((a, b) => b.start - a.start)
   111→  for (const { start, end } of removals) {
   112→    description = description.slice(0, start) + description.slice(end)
   113→  }
   114→  description = description.replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim()
   115→
   116→  if (!description) return null
   117→
   118→  return { description, amount, date }
   119→}
   120→```

    77→

    88→    return sendMessage(chatId, "Arquivo muito grande. Máximo 5MB.")
    89→  }
    90→
    91→  try {
    92→    // Download file
    93→    const filePath = await getFile(doc.file_id)
    94→    if (!filePath) {
    95→      return sendMessage(chatId, "Erro ao acessar o arquivo.")
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    const content = await downloadFile(filePath)
    99→
   100→    // Parse CSV
   101→    const origin = "Telegram"
   102→    const parsed = await parseCSV(content, origin)
   103→
   104→    if (parsed.length === 0) {
   105→      return sendMessage(chatId, "Nenhuma transação encontrada no arquivo.")
   106→    }
   107→
   108→    // Check for duplicates
   109→    let duplicateCount = 0
   110→    const uniqueTransactions: typeof parsed = []

   112→    for (const t of parsed) {
   113→      const amount = t.type === "EXPENSE" ? -Math.abs(t.amount) : Math.abs(t.amount)
   114→      const dup = await findDuplicate({
   115→        userId,
   116→        description: t.description,
   117→        amount,
   118→        date: t.date,
   119→      })
   120→      if (dup) {
   121→        duplicateCount++
   122→      } else {
   123→        uniqueTransactions.push(t)
   124→      }
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    if (uniqueTransactions.length === 0) {
   128→      return sendMessage(
   129→        chatId,
   130→        `Todas as ${parsed.length} transações já existem no sistema.`
   131→      )
   132→    }

   134→    // Store for confirmation in database

   152→          type: t.type || "EXPENSE",
   153→        }))),

   156→      },
   157→    })

   162→    ]

   202→
   203→  const transactions = JSON.parse(pending.payload) as Array<{
   204→    description: string
   205→    amount: number
   206→    date: string | Date
   207→    categoryId: string | null
   208→    isInstallment: boolean
   209→    currentInstallment: number | null
   210→    totalInstallments: number | null
   211→    type: string
   212→  }>
   213→
   214→  let importedCount = 0
   215→  for (const t of transactions) {
   216→    const type = t.type || "EXPENSE"
   217→    let amount = t.amount
   218→    if (type === "EXPENSE" && amount > 0) amount = -amount
   219→    else if (type === "INCOME" && amount < 0) amount = Math.abs(amount)
   220→
   221→    const dateStr = typeof t.date === "string" && !(t.date as string).includes("T")

   244→  return editMessageText(

   246→    messageId,

   279→3. `src/lib/utils.ts` (full file) - formatCurrency
   280→4. `src/lib/telegram/client.ts` (full file) - sendMessage, InlineKeyboardButton
   281→
   282→**Pre-conditions**:
   283→- [ ] `src/lib/telegram/` directory exists
   284→- [ ] Prisma client available via `@/lib/db`
   285→
   286→**Why**: Três consultas core do bot: resumo mensal (totais + orçamento geral), gastos por categoria (vs orçamento), e últimas 10 transações.
   287→
   288→**Acceptance Criteria**:
   289→```gherkin
   290→Given a linked user requests summary
   291→When handleSummaryQuery is called
   292→Then it returns formatted text with income, expense, balance, and budget usage
   293→
   294→Given a linked user requests category breakdown

   296→Then it returns each category with spent amount vs budget (if defined)
   297→

   306→```typescript
   307→import prisma from "@/lib/db"
   308→import { formatCurrency } from "@/lib/utils"
   309→import { sendMessage, type InlineKeyboardButton } from "./client"
   310→
   311→export async function handleSummaryQuery(chatId: number, userId: string) {
   312→  const now = new Date()
   313→  const month = now.getMonth() + 1
   314→  const year = now.getFullYear()
   315→
   316→  const monthStart = new Date(year, month - 1, 1)
   317→  const monthEnd = new Date(year, month, 0, 23, 59, 59, 999)
   318→
   319→  // Fetch transactions for current month
   320→  const transactions = await prisma.transaction.findMany({
   321→    where: {
   322→      userId,
   323→      date: { gte: monthStart, lte: monthEnd },
   324→      deletedAt: null,
   325→    },
   326→    include: { investmentTransaction: true },
   327→  })
   328→
   329→  // Calculate totals (same logic as /api/summary)
   330→  let income = 0
   331→  let expense = 0
   332→  for (const t of transactions) {
   333→    if ((t as Record<string, unknown>).investmentTransaction) continue
   334→    if (t.type === "TRANSFER") continue
   335→    if (t.type === "INCOME") income += Math.abs(t.amount)
   336→    else if (t.type === "EXPENSE") expense += Math.abs(t.amount)
   337→  }
   338→  const balance = income - expense
   339→
   340→  // Get total budget
   341→  const budgets = await prisma.budget.findMany({
   342→    where: { userId, isActive: true },
   343→  })
   344→  const totalBudget = budgets.reduce((sum, b) => sum + b.amount, 0)
   345→
   346→  const monthName = monthStart.toLocaleDateString("pt-BR", { month: "long", year: "numeric" })
   347→
   348→  const lines = [
   349→    `📊 Resumo - ${monthName}`,
   350→    "",
   351→    `Despesas:  ${formatCurrency(expense)}`,
   352→    `Receitas:  ${formatCurrency(income)}`,
   353→    `Saldo:     ${formatCurrency(balance)}`,
   354→  ]
   355→
   356→  if (totalBudget > 0) {
   357→    const pct = Math.round((expense / totalBudget) * 100)
   358→    const filled = Math.round(pct / 10)
   359→    const bar = "█".repeat(Math.min(filled, 10)) + "░".repeat(Math.max(10 - filled, 0))
   360→    lines.push("")
   361→    lines.push(`Orçamento: ${formatCurrency(totalBudget)}`)
   362→    lines.push(`Usado:     ${pct}% ${bar}`)
   363→  }
   364→
   365→  return sendMessage(chatId, lines.join("\n"), {
   366→    reply_markup: {
   367→      inline_keyboard: [[{ text: "🔙 Menu", callback_data: "menu:0" }]],
   368→    },
   369→  })
   370→}
   371→
   372→export async function handleCategoryQuery(chatId: number, userId: string) {
   373→  const now = new Date()
   374→  const month = now.getMonth() + 1
   375→  const year = now.getFullYear()
   376→
   377→  const monthStart = new Date(year, month - 1, 1)
   378→  const monthEnd = new Date(year, month, 0, 23, 59, 59, 999)
   379→
   380→  // Fetch expense transactions with categories
   381→  const transactions = await prisma.transaction.findMany({
   382→    where: {
   383→      userId,
   384→      type: "EXPENSE",
   385→      date: { gte: monthStart, lte: monthEnd },
   386→      deletedAt: null,
   387→    },
   388→    include: { category: true },
   389→  })
   390→
   391→  // Aggregate by category
   392→  const categoryTotals = new Map<string, { name: string; total: number }>()
   393→  for (const t of transactions) {
   394→    const catName = t.category?.name || "Sem categoria"
   395→    const catId = t.categoryId || "none"
   396→    const existing = categoryTotals.get(catId) || { name: catName, total: 0 }
   397→    existing.total += Math.abs(t.amount)
   398→    categoryTotals.set(catId, existing)
   399→  }
   400→
   401→  // Fetch budgets
   402→  const budgets = await prisma.budget.findMany({
   403→    where: { userId, isActive: true },
   404→    include: { category: true },
   405→  })
   406→  const budgetMap = new Map(budgets.map(b => [b.categoryId, b.amount]))
   407→
   408→  const monthName = monthStart.toLocaleDateString("pt-BR", { month: "long", year: "numeric" })
   409→
   410→  const lines = [`🏷️ Gastos por categoria - ${monthName}`, ""]
   411→
   412→  // Sort by total descending
   413→  const sorted = [...categoryTotals.entries()].sort((a, b) => b[1].total - a[1].total)
   414→
   415→  for (const [catId, { name, total }] of sorted) {
   416→    const budget = budgetMap.get(catId)
   417→    if (budget) {
   418→      const pct = Math.round((total / budget) * 100)
   419→      const warning = pct >= 100 ? " ⚠️" : ""
   420→      lines.push(`${name}: ${formatCurrency(total)} / ${formatCurrency(budget)} (${pct}%)${warning}`)
   421→    } else {
   422→      lines.push(`${name}: ${formatCurrency(total)}`)
   423→    }
   424→  }
   425→
   426→  if (sorted.length === 0) {
   427→    lines.push("Nenhuma despesa neste mês.")
   428→  }
   429→
   430→  return sendMessage(chatId, lines.join("\n"), {
   431→    reply_markup: {
   432→      inline_keyboard: [[{ text: "🔙 Menu", callback_data: "menu:0" }]],
   433→    },
   434→  })
   435→}
   436→
   437→export async function handleTransactionsQuery(
   438→  chatId: number,
   439→  userId: string,
   440→  offset: number = 0
   441→) {
   442→  const limit = 10
   443→  const transactions = await prisma.transaction.findMany({
   444→    where: {
   445→      userId,
   446→      deletedAt: null,
   447→    },
   448→    include: { category: true },
   449→    orderBy: { date: "desc" },
   450→    skip: offset,
   451→    take: limit + 1, // Fetch one extra to check if there are more
   452→  })
   453→
   454→  const hasMore = transactions.length > limit
   455→  const display = transactions.slice(0, limit)
   456→
   457→  const lines = [`📋 ${offset === 0 ? "Últimas" : "Mais"} transações`, ""]
   458→
   459→  for (const t of display) {
   460→    const date = new Date(t.date).toLocaleDateString("pt-BR", {
   461→      day: "2-digit",
   462→      month: "2-digit",
   463→    })
   464→    const sign = t.type === "INCOME" ? "+" : "-"
   465→    const amount = formatCurrency(Math.abs(t.amount))
   466→    const cat = t.category?.name || ""
   467→    lines.push(`${date} ${t.description.substring(0, 25)}  ${sign}${amount}  ${cat}`)
   468→  }
   469→
   470→  if (display.length === 0) {
   471→    lines.push("Nenhuma transação encontrada.")
   472→  }
   473→
   474→  const keyboard: InlineKeyboardButton[][] = []
   475→  const navRow: InlineKeyboardButton[] = []
   476→  if (offset > 0) {
   477→    navRow.push({ text: "⬅️ Anteriores", callback_data: `txns:${Math.max(0, offset - limit)}` })
   478→  }
   479→  if (hasMore) {
   480→    navRow.push({ text: "➡️ Próximas", callback_data: `txns:${offset + limit}` })
   481→  }
   482→  if (navRow.length > 0) keyboard.push(navRow)
   483→  keyboard.push([{ text: "🔙 Menu", callback_data: "menu:0" }])
   484→
   485→  return sendMessage(chatId, lines.join("\n"), {
   486→    reply_markup: { inline_keyboard: keyboard },
   487→  })
   488→}
   489→```
   490→
   491→**Verification**: `npm run build`
   492→
   493→**On Failure**:
   494→- If `investmentTransaction` relation not found: Check Transaction model includes this relation; may need to filter differently
   495→- If formatCurrency import fails: Verify `@/lib/utils` path and export name
   496→
   497→---
   498→

## Comprehensive Summary: Telegram Bot Feature for MyPocket

The Telegram bot integration plan is a **multi-phase implementation** to add chat-based expense management to MyPocket. Here's what this feature encompasses:

### **Overview & Goals**

The bot enables users to register expenses, import bank statement CSVs, and query financial summaries directly from Telegram—without leaving the app. It includes:
- **Account linking** via secure deep links (10-minute expiring tokens)
- **Duplicate prevention** across web and Telegram imports
- **Smart categorization** of expenses automatically
- **CSV import** with duplicate detection
- **Financial queries** (monthly summary, category breakdown, recent transactions)

### **Architecture**

**Core Components:**

1. **Prisma Schema Extensions** (Phase 1)
   - `TelegramLink`: Maps user account to Telegram chat ID
   - `TelegramLinkToken`: Temporary tokens for account linking (10-min expiry)
   - `TelegramPendingImport`: Stores pending CSV imports for confirmation workflow

2. **Deduplication Module** (`src/lib/dedup.ts`)
   - Centralized transaction duplicate detection using exact matching (case-insensitive description + amount + same-day date)
   - Works across web imports and bot transactions
   - Conservatively designed (false positives are rare)

3. **Telegram Integration** (Phases 2-5)
   - **Client wrapper** (`src/lib/telegram/client.ts`): Abstraction over Telegram Bot API
   - **Bot handler** (`src/lib/telegram/bot.ts`): Routes incoming updates to command handlers
   - **Webhook endpoint** (`src/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`): Receives updates from Telegram
   - **Setup endpoint** (`src/app/api/telegram/setup/route.ts`): Configures webhook URL
   - **Link API** (`src/app/api/telegram/link/route.ts`): Generates/revokes linking tokens

### **Key Capabilities by Phase**

| Phase | Feature | Details |
|-------|---------|---------|
| **1** | Schema + Dedup | Prisma models + centralized duplicate detection module + integration into web import |
| **2** | Bot Core + Webhook | Telegram client, bot handler, webhook endpoint, stub command handlers |
| **3** | Account Linking | Deep link flow, `/start TOKEN` command, Settings UI tab for linking/unlinking |
| **4** | Expense Registration | Message parser (freeform text → transaction), inline keyboard confirmation, category selection, transaction creation |
| **5** | CSV Import + Queries | CSV file upload/download/parse, three query commands (/menu with summary/categories/transactions) |

### **User Workflows**

**Linking Account:**
1. User goes to Settings → Telegram tab
2. Clicks "Vincular Telegram" button
3. Gets a deep link (expires in 10 min)
4. Clicks link to open Telegram bot
5. Bot validates token, creates link, responds with success

**Registering Expense:**
1. User sends message: `PIX RESTAURANTE FULANO 45,90 05/03/2026`
2. Bot parses text → extracts description, amount, date
3. Bot auto-categorizes based on keywords
4. Bot sends confirmation with inline buttons: [✅ Confirmar] [✏️ Categoria] [❌ Cancelar]
5. User clicks Confirmar → transaction created

**Importing CSV:**
1. User sends .csv file
2. Bot downloads, parses (detects bank type: C6, Itaú, BTG)
3. Checks for duplicates
4. Shows preview: "10 transações encontradas, 2 duplicatas ignoradas, 8 prontas"
5. Offers [✅ Importar] [❌ Cancelar]
6. On Importar → creates all 8 transactions, shows success

**Querying Data:**
1. User sends `/menu`
2. Bot shows buttons: [📈 Resumo] [🏷️ Categorias] [📋 Transações]
3. Clicking Resumo → shows current month summary (income, expenses, balance, budget usage bar)
4. Clicking Categorias → shows spending per category vs budget limits
5. Clicking Transações → shows last 10 transactions with pagination

### **Technical Highlights**

- **No external dependencies** for Telegram: Uses native `fetch` to Telegram Bot API
- **Webhook security**: Requests validated via `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` header
- **Middleware exception**: `/api/telegram/webhook` bypasses JWT auth to allow unauthenticated Telegram requests
- **Message parsing**: Regex-based parser handles Brazilian number format (1.234,56), optional dates, flexible word order
- **Callback data encoding**: Uses compact formats (e.g., `confirm:categoryId`) since Telegram limits callback data to 64 bytes
- **Database queries**: Reuses existing logic from web app (`summary` endpoint patterns, `categorizer` module, `csv-parser`)
- **Duplicate check**: Same-day matching (ignores time) to catch real duplicates without false positives

### **Success Criteria**

- [ ] Schema generates without errors (`npm run db:generate`)
- [ ] Sending CSV twice (web + bot) → second import reports duplicates skipped
- [ ] Deep link generated in Settings → opens Telegram bot
- [ ] `/start TOKEN` links account → bot responds "Conta vinculada"
- [ ] Sending `PIX RESTAURANTE 45,90 05/03/2026` → transaction appears in dashboard
- [ ] CSV import flow shows summary → confirms → creates transactions
- [ ] `/menu` shows three query options → each displays correct data

### **Related Documentation**

- `ai_docs/research/2026-03-07-telegram-bot-research.md` — Detailed codebase analysis
- `ai_docs/brainstorm/2026-03-07-telegram-bot-design.md` — Design decisions and validation
- `ai_docs/diagrams/` — Architecture diagrams (data models, API flows, component hierarchy)

**Status**: All 5 phases are planned; phases 1-2 marked complete in Phase 2 documentation with learnings captured.

4. Bot sends confirmation with inline buttons: [✅ Confirmar] [✏️ Categoria] [❌ Cancelar]
5. User clicks Confirmar → transaction created

**Importing CSV:**
1. User sends .csv file
2. Bot downloads, parses (detects bank type: C6, Itaú, BTG)
3. Checks for duplicates
4. Shows preview: "10 transações encontradas, 2 duplicatas ignoradas, 8 prontas"
5. Offers [✅ Importar] [❌ Cancelar]
6. On Importar → creates all 8 transactions, shows success

**Querying Data:**
1. User sends `/menu`
2. Bot shows buttons: [📈 Resumo] [🏷️ Categorias] [📋 Transações]
3. Clicking Resumo → shows current month summary (income, expenses, balance, budget usage bar)
4. Clicking Categorias → shows spending per category vs budget limits
5. Clicking Transações → shows last 10 transactions with pagination

### **Technical Highlights**

- **No external dependencies** for Telegram: Uses native `fetch` to Telegram Bot API
- **Webhook security**: Requests validated via `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` header
- **Middleware exception**: `/api/telegram/webhook` bypasses JWT auth to allow unauthenticated Telegram requests
- **Message parsing**: Regex-based parser handles Brazilian number format (1.234,56), optional dates, flexible word order
- **Callback data encoding**: Uses compact formats (e.g., `confirm:categoryId`) since Telegram limits callback data to 64 bytes
- **Database queries**: Reuses existing logic from web app (`summary` endpoint patterns, `categorizer` module, `csv-parser`)
- **Duplicate check**: Same-day matching (ignores time) to catch real duplicates without false positives

### **Success Criteria**

- [ ] Schema generates without errors (`npm run db:generate`)
- [ ] Sending CSV twice (web + bot) → second import reports duplicates skipped
- [ ] Deep link generated in Settings → opens Telegram bot
- [ ] `/start TOKEN` links account → bot responds "Conta vinculada"
- [ ] Sending `PIX RESTAURANTE 45,90 05/03/2026` → transaction appears in dashboard
- [ ] CSV import flow shows summary → confirms → creates transactions
- [ ] `/menu` shows three query options → each displays correct data

### **Related Documentation**

- `ai_docs/research/2026-03-07-telegram-bot-research.md` — Detailed codebase analysis
- `ai_docs/brainstorm/2026-03-07-telegram-bot-design.md` — Design decisions and validation
- `ai_docs/diagrams/` — Architecture diagrams (data models, API flows, component hierarchy)

**Status**: All 5 phases are planned; phases 1-2 marked complete in Phase 2 documentation with learnings captured.

# Comunicação de Novas Features — WhatsApp

Diretrizes e templates para mensagens de lançamento de features enviadas via WhatsApp aos usuários do MyPocket.

---

## Diretrizes

### Tom e Estilo
- **Friendly e acessível** — como se fosse um amigo contando uma novidade, não um comunicado corporativo
- **Convidar a testar** — sempre incluir um CTA claro pedindo feedback ou convidando a experimentar
- Usar "você" (nunca "prezado cliente" ou linguagem formal)
- Manter entusiasmo sem exagero — 1 a 2 emojis por bloco, não mais
- Frases curtas e diretas. Evitar parágrafos longos

### Estrutura da Mensagem
1. **Abertura** — Nome do app + título da novidade (1 linha, com emoji)
2. **Separador visual** — Linha de ━ para dar respiro
3. **Blocos de funcionalidade** — Cada feature em um bloco com emoji + título em bold + descrição curta (2-3 linhas)
4. **Como ativar/usar** — Passos numerados, simples e diretos
5. **Separador visual**
6. **Fechamento** — Frase de contexto (sincronização, segurança, etc.)
7. **CTA** — Convite para testar + pedido de feedback

### Formatação WhatsApp
- Bold: `*texto*`
- Itálico: `_texto_`
- Riscado: `~texto~`
- Monospace: ``` `texto` ``` ou blocos com três crases
- Sem suporte a links clicáveis com texto âncora — usar URL direta quando necessário
- Sem suporte a headers (#), tabelas ou markdown avançado
- Listas com • (bullet unicode) ou números
- Separadores com ━ (unicode box drawing)

### Boas Práticas
- Máximo ~20 linhas visíveis (WhatsApp trunca mensagens longas com "Ler mais")
- Testar a mensagem no próprio WhatsApp antes de enviar em massa
- Incluir exemplo prático quando a feature envolve input do usuário
- Sempre mencionar que a feature já está disponível (senão o usuário espera)
- Fechar com pergunta ou convite — gera resposta e engajamento

---

## Template Base

```
*🚀 MyPocket — [Título da Novidade]*

[Frase de abertura — 1 linha, friendly, dizendo o que mudou]

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

*[emoji] [Nome da funcionalidade 1]*
[Descrição em 2-3 linhas. Incluir exemplo se aplicável.]

*[emoji] [Nome da funcionalidade 2]*
[Descrição em 2-3 linhas.]

*[emoji] Como ativar*
1. [Passo 1]
2. [Passo 2]
3. [Passo 3]

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

[Frase de contexto — segurança, sincronização, etc.]

[CTA — convite para testar + pedido de feedback]
```

---

## Mensagens Enviadas

### Bot do Telegram (março/2026)

```
*🚀 MyPocket — Novidade: Bot do Telegram*

Agora dá pra registrar gastos, importar extratos e consultar suas finanças *direto pelo Telegram*. Sem precisar abrir o app!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

*💬 Registre gastos por mensagem*
Mande algo como:
_PIX Restaurante 45,90_
O bot categoriza automaticamente e pede sua confirmação. Um toque e tá lançado!

*📎 Importe extratos pelo chat*
Envie o CSV do seu banco (C6, Itaú ou BTG) direto no chat. O bot detecta duplicatas, mostra um resumo e importa tudo pra você.

*📊 Consulte suas finanças*
Use /menu no bot pra ver:
• Resumo do mês (receitas, despesas, saldo)
• Gastos por categoria vs. orçamento
• Últimas transações

*🔗 Como ativar*
1. Acesse _Configurações → Telegram_ no MyPocket
2. Clique em _Vincular Telegram_
3. Abra o link e pronto — conta conectada!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Tudo fica sincronizado. O que você lançar pelo Telegram aparece no dashboard, e se importar o mesmo extrato duas vezes, o bot já ignora as duplicatas.

Testa lá e me conta o que achou! Se tiver qualquer dúvida ou sugestão, é só responder aqui 👇
```
